chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH CIRCUIT COURT MIRPURKHAS Constitutional Petition No.S-204 of 2024 (Haji Sher Khan vs. P.O Sindh and others) DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE Date of hearing and order 24-09-2024. Mr. Farhan Ahmed Bozdar advocate for the petitioner a/w petitioner. Mr. Sikander Ali Kolachi, advocate for respondent No.08. Mr. Dhani Bakhsh Mari, Assistant P.G a/w SIP Kamran Ali Halepoto SHO P.S Town Mirpurkhas, ASI Ameer Bux of P S Khipro and HC Saleem Ahmed of CIA Mirpurkhas. = ORDER Adnan-ul-Karim Memon, J. Petitioner Haji Sher Khan through instant petition is seeking directions to the official respondents not to cause harassment to him at the behest of private respondents and to provide protection to him. 2. The learned counsel for the private respondents and learned A.P.G with one voice stated that neither they caused harassment to the petitioner nor they intend to do so in the future. 3. The learned counsel for the private respondents submitted that petitioner Haji Sher Khan and one Zafar Khan fraudulently sold them 13 plots in the Qazi Faiz Residency Housing Scheme in Khipro. They paid a total of Rs. 10, 00,000/- for these plots in 2020. Despite repeated promises, petitioner has failed to execute registered sale deeds for the plots. The petitioner has also issued threats and attempted to forcibly evict the respondents from the property. 4. In view of the above, this petition is disposed of with the direction that no harassment shall be caused to the petitioner and police shall be neutral in private affairs; however, if any cognizable offense is committed police shall be free to take action under law.

The petitioner has also issued threats and attempted to forcibly evict the respondents from the property. 4. In view of the above, this petition is disposed of with the direction that no harassment shall be caused to the petitioner and police shall be neutral in private affairs; however, if any cognizable offense is committed police shall be free to take action under law. If the parties feel harassed by police officials, they have to approach the D.I.G.P concerned and their grievances will be thoroughly investigated, and the D.I.G.P concerned will issue a detailed order regarding the findings after hearing them. JUDGE
